Notice of Half-Year Results and Trading Update, and Details of Investor Presentation via Investor Meet Company
Litigation Capital Management Limited ("LCM" or the "Company"), an alternative asset manager specialising in dispute financing solutions internationally, will announce its results for the six months ended 31 December 2025 on Tuesday, 31 March 2026.
Furthermore, the Company is pleased to announce that it will provide a live presentation relating to the Interim Results via Investor Meet Company on 31 Mar 2026, 09:00 BST.
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted pre-event via your Investor Meet Company dashboard up until 30 Mar 2026, 09:00 BST, or at any time during the live presentation.
Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and add to meet Litigation Capital Management Limited via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/litigation-capital-management-limited/register-investor
Investors who already follow Litigation Capital Management Limited on the Investor Meet Company platform will automatically be invited.
Enquiries
Litigation Capital Management
Patrick Moloney, Chief Executive Officer
David Collins, Chief Financial Officer

latest.title: Judgment Delivered in Commercial Litigation Claim
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8002W
Litigation Capital Management Ltd
17 March 2026
17 March 2026
Litigation Capital Management Limited
("
LCM
" or the "
Company
")
Judgment Delivered in Commercial Litigation Claim
Litigation Capital Management Limited ("LCM" or the "Company"), an alternative asset manager specialising in dispute financing solutions internationally, announces that an Australian Court has delivered judgment in a commercial litigation claim funded by the Company.
On 11 March 2026, LCM announced that it anticipated a judgment on a small case investment. This judgment has now been delivered, with the Court finding against LCM's funded party.
LCM funded the case with A$1.4 million in shareholder invested capital. A policy of ATE insurance is in place to protect against adverse costs risk.
LCM is currently reviewing the judgment and assessing potential next steps alongside the funded party and legal representatives.
Enquiries

David Collins, Chief Financial Officer

latest.title: Update on Case Investment and Strategic Review
latest.content:
Today 09:34
RNS Number : 2269W
Litigation Capital Management Ltd
11 March 2026
11 March 2026
Litigation Capital Management Limited
("
LCM
" or the "
Company
")
Update on Case Investment and Strategic Review
Litigation Capital Management Limited ("LCM" or the "Company"), an alternative asset manager specialising in dispute financing solutions internationally, is pleased to announce that its funded party has been successful in the High Court of Australia in a trademark dispute claim it funded on behalf of Katie Perry (Designer), a Sydney-based fashion designer against Katy Perry (Singer) and her related corporate entities.
The claim was initially brought in the Federal Court of Australia where there was a finding of infringement of the Designer's trademark by the Singer and one of her related corporate entities. The Singer and her related corporate entities appealed this decision to the Full Court of the Federal Court who found, among other things, that the Designer's trademark should be cancelled as she had applied for the trademark knowing of the Singer's reputation. The Designer applied for special leave to appeal to the High Court of Australia which was granted on 11 April 2025. Following a hearing before the High Court of Australia on 9 September 2025, by majority, the appeal was successful.
The matter will now go back to the Full Court in respect of outstanding issues, including costs of the proceeding before the primary judge and the Full Court. Following that, the damages need to be quantified and at that point we will be able to determine LCM's return on investment. LCM has invested A$3.3m of shareholder capital into this claim (it is a balance sheet investment without any participation of fund investors) and it has been held in LCM's financial statements at a value of A$3.3m.
The Company also anticipates a judgment on another small case investment, with A$1.4 million shareholder invested capital, within the next week. The outcome will be announced when the judgment has been handed down.
LCM will be finalising the date of its half-year results presentation in the coming weeks, and this will then be communicated to investors. The presentation is expected to be later than usual, around the end of March, in order to increase the likelihood of being able to give shareholders a meaningful update on the outcome of the Strategic Review.
The Company will make further announcements as and when appropriate.
Patrick Moloney, CEO of LCM, commented:
"LCM has provided funding for this claim from its balance sheet since 2019. It is a classic instance of disputes finance enabling a claimant to bring a claim which they would otherwise not have the resources to fund. The case was successful at first instance, then overturned on appeal and now the case has succeeded in the High Court. We now look forward to pursuing this claim to conclusion."
Enquiries
Litigation Capital Management
Patrick Moloney, Chief Executive Officer
David Collins, Chief Financial Officer
Cavendish (Nomad and Broker)
Tel: 020 7220 0500
Jonny Franklin-Adams and Isaac Hooper (Corporate Finance)
Ella Bedford (Corporate Broking)

latest.title: Covenant Waiver Extension
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8208U
Litigation Capital Management Ltd
02 March 2026
2 March 2026
Litigation Capital Management Limited
("
LCM
" or the "
Company
")
Covenant Waiver Extension
Litigation Capital Management Limited ("LCM"), an alternative asset manager specialising in dispute financing solutions internationally, announces that the debt covenant waiver from Northleaf that was due to expire at the end of February 2026 has been extended to 15 April 2026. During the extended waiver period the interest on the loan remains increased by 2.00% per annum consistent with the initial waiver. There is no additional one-time waiver fee in relation to this extension.
The Strategic Review that was first announced on 15 September 2025 continues to progress and this waiver extension is reflective of Northleaf's ongoing support while LCM works towards a long-term resolution of its capital position. LCM management will provide an update on the progress of the Strategic Review in due course.
Enquiries

latest.title: Increase in Credit Facility and Waiver Extension
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 2024R
Litigation Capital Management Ltd
02 February 2026
2 February 2026
Litigation Capital Management Limited
("
LCM
" or the "
Company
")
Increase in Credit Facility and Covenant Waiver Extension
Litigation Capital Management Limited ("LCM"), an alternative asset manager specialising in dispute financing solutions internationally, announces that it has to increase its credit facility with funds managed by Northleaf Capital Partners (Canada) Ltd ("Northleaf") from US$75 million to US$100 million. Furthermore, the debt covenant waiver from Northleaf that was due to expire at the end of January 2026 has been extended to 1 March 2026. During the extended waiver period the interest on the loan remains increased by 2.00% per annum consistent with the initial waiver. There is no additional one-time waiver fee in relation to this extension.
The Strategic Review that was first announced on 15 September 2025 continues to progress and this waiver extension is reflective of Northleaf's ongoing support while LCM works towards a long-term resolution of its capital position. LCM management will provide an update on the progress of the Strategic Review in due course.
Enquiries

company_30s.what_they_do: Litigation Capital Management Limited funds lawsuits for plaintiffs, collecting a share of the settlement or judgment. They act as a third‑party investor, providing capital that plaintiffs can use to pursue claims while the firm takes on the legal risk.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Litigation finance is a niche but growing alternative asset class. It offers investors exposure to potentially high‑value legal disputes without needing to understand the law. The company’s focus on commercial and class‑action cases places it in a market that is expanding as more jurisdictions allow third‑party funding.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still heavily dependent on case outcomes. Recent filings show a mix of positive judgments and cost orders, but no clear revenue growth yet. The stock sits near the bottom of its historical range, indicating a prolonged period of low activity and limited cash flow.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy updating on ongoing litigation. Recent notices cover a major judgment, a covenant waiver, and a quarterly results announcement. These filings suggest that management is trying to keep the board and shareholders informed while the company remains in a waiting‑room for case outcomes.

The narrative can be boiled down to two threads: (1) legal results – the company’s core engine – and (2) financial updates – a mix of cash‑flow statements and covenant extensions that keep the firm afloat.

While the updates are routine, there are hints that the firm is stabilising: a positive judgment in March and a covenant waiver extending the funding window show that the company is still able to secure the capital needed to keep cases alive.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism amid a litigation grind
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders point to the recent judgment win and the possibility of additional funding rounds. They argue that a successful case could unlock a sizeable payout that would lift the stock.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: The majority of chatter highlights the high cost of litigation, the risk of adverse costs orders, and the fact that the company’s valuation is still very low. Bears fear that a single adverse ruling could wipe out the firm’s remaining assets.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from flat to slightly hopeful after the March judgment, but overall engagement remains low. The market is still waiting for a catalyst that will break the current silence.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat, meaning there is no public buzz. This lack of noise is a signal that a surprise event could create a big move.

near_term_catalysts: A positive court ruling or a favorable settlement in the next RNS (likely mid‑April).
An announcement of additional funding or a new client partnership that could improve cash flow.
A spike in trading volume that exceeds the 30‑day average, indicating renewed investor interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden jump in daily volume coupled with a bullish headline. Pay close attention to any mention of a settlement amount or a change in the firm’s debt covenants. A clear statement that a case will close within the next 30 days is a strong confirmation signal.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early to mid‑phase of a crash‑recovery cycle. The APEX score indicates a tight price compression, suggesting that a breakout could come soon. The key is to wait for a catalyst that confirms the market’s belief that the litigation outcome is in the off‑tape.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company sits at the bottom of a deep crash. Historically, such bottoms have rebounded sharply when a case turns in the firm’s favour. A single positive judgment could lift the price back to 50% of its pre‑crash level, offering a 200% upside. The APEX score signals tight range‑bound behaviour, so a breakout could be decisive.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the next court decision is negative or if the firm is forced to extend its covenant further, the stock could stay near the floor or drop further. The worst realistic outcome is a continued slide to the current floor, which would mean a loss of the remaining equity value.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: near the current floor (essentially 0% equity value). Potential gain: a 200% upside if the stock reaches half its previous high. The asymmetry is clear – the downside is limited to the floor, while the upside is substantial.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a volume spike that breaks the 30‑day average, ideally with a positive RNS headline. Size the position to 10% of your trading capital, set a stop 20% below the breakout level to protect against a false move, and hold until the price shows sustained momentum.
playbook.if_watching: Trigger a buy if you see a court decision announced or a funding round secured. The entry price should be near the current market level, with a tight stop to limit exposure until the catalyst materialises.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has emerged, so the setup remains bullish. If a bankruptcy filing or delisting notice appears, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash has left LIT.L at a low floor, and a forthcoming positive litigation outcome could trigger a sharp rebound, offering a high upside with limited downside.
